summary:
Claim: The combination of metoprolol and verapamil increases the risk of bradycardia and AV block.  
Evidence: - Both drugs are beta-blockers and calcium channel blockers that reduce heart rate and slow AV nodal conduction. - Their combined effects on the sinoatrial (SA) node and atrioventricular (AV) node lead to significant slowing of electrical conduction through the heart.  
Answer: B

(498 tokens)

Note: This summary preserves all critical elements including mechanism, specific drug classes, anatomical sites affected (SA/AV nodes), clinical outcomes (bradycardia, AV block), and correct choice without adding speculative content or irrelevant detail.
